item.title: pkg/v2.6.21: enhance: [2.6] update knowhere version (#51766) | Release notes from milvus
item.description: issue: #42937/kind branch-featureSummaryUpdate Knowhere dependency from v2.6.17 to v2.6.18.Use the Knowhere v2.6.18 tag from the 2.6 branch:https://github.com/zilliztech/knowhere/tree/v2.6.18Changes i
item.content:encoded: issue: #42937/kind branch-featureSummaryUpdate Knowhere dependency from v2.6.17 to v2.6.18.Use the Knowhere v2.6.18 tag from the 2.6 branch:https://github.com/zilliztech/knowhere/tree/v2.6.18Changes included in v2.6.18 (since v2.6.17)fix: handle int8 cosine normalization for GPU CAGRAzilliztech/knowhere#1724)Note: this is a 2.6-branch-specific dependency bump; knowhere 2.6 isTest plan git diff --check upstream/2.6..HEAD git ls-remote --tags https://github.com/zilliztech/knowhere.git refs/tags/v2

item.title: My agent cloned GitHub (sort of) using the Neon backend | Blog — Neon Docs
item.description: GitHub has been down a lot lately. During one of those episodes, I had the idea of asking my agent to use our new backend suite (still in beta) to build a working Git host. This is genuinely outside o
item.content:encoded: GitHub has been down a lot lately. During one of those episodes, I had the idea of asking my agent to use our new backend suite (still in beta) to build a working Git host. This is genuinely outside of my comfort zone as I had no prior knowledge of Git server internals, so I didn't have much hopes, but it went surprisingly well...
item.author: Roman Zaynetdinov

_custom.originalTitle: My agent cloned GitHub (sort of) using the Neon backend

item.title: Debug your Postgres from the terminal: a tour of `neon inspect db` | Blog — Neon Docs
item.description: The Neon CLI now ships `neon inspect db`: read-only, high-signal Postgres diagnostics that answer "what is slow?" without leaving the terminal or writing a single catalog query. Each subcommand runs o
item.content:encoded: The Neon CLI now ships `neon inspect db`: read-only, high-signal Postgres diagnostics that answer "what is slow?" without leaving the terminal or writing a single catalog query. Each subcommand runs one known-good query against Postgres' own statistics and catalog views and prints a clean table (or JSON/YAML for scripting).
item.author: Savannah Longoria

_custom.originalTitle: Debug your Postgres from the terminal: a tour of `neon inspect db`

item.title: Context assembly: building the prompt the model actually sees | Redis Blog
item.description: The prompt a production LLM receives is almost never something a person wrote. By the time a request reaches the model, your app has stitched together system instructions, retrieved documents, convers
item.content:encoded: The prompt a production LLM receives is almost never something a person wrote. By the time a request reaches the model, your app has stitched together system instructions, retrieved documents, conversation history, tool schemas, and stored memories in...
item.author: Jeff Mills

_custom.originalTitle: Context assembly: building the prompt the model actually sees

item.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) vs. Agent2Agent (A2A): which protocol do you need? | Redis Blog
item.description: Somewhere around your third agent, someone in a design review asks, "Shouldn't we be using A2A for this?" It's a fair question that most teams can't answer well, because the two big agent protocols ke
item.content:encoded: Somewhere around your third agent, someone in a design review asks, "Shouldn't we be using A2A for this?" It's a fair question that most teams can't answer well, because the two big agent protocols keep getting lumped together when they solve differen...
item.author: Jeff Mills

_custom.originalTitle: Model Context Protocol (MCP) vs. Agent2Agent (A2A): which protocol do you need?

item.title: 5 agent architecture scenarios: assess MCP vs. A2A | Redis Blog
item.description: We've watched enterprise teams go from vague "we might do agent stuff" conversations to full internal agent environments in a matter of months, and the same protocol question comes up in almost every
item.content:encoded: We've watched enterprise teams go from vague "we might do agent stuff" conversations to full internal agent environments in a matter of months, and the same protocol question comes up in almost every one: does the design need the Model Context Protoco...
item.author: Jeff Mills

_custom.originalTitle: 5 agent architecture scenarios: assess MCP vs. A2A

item.title: Agentic infrastructure with clickhousectl: a distributed ClickStack across three continents | ClickHouse Blog
item.description: How a coding agent used clickhousectl to stand up a distributed ClickStack across the US, Europe, and Japan, with regional ingestion and one global view.

item.title: Benchmarking the ClickStack MCP Server with hdx-evals | ClickHouse Blog
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at hdx-evals, the open-source framework we built to benchmark the ClickStack MCP server against a raw SQL baseline using deterministic synthetic incidents, sandboxed Claude ag
item.content:encoded: A behind-the-scenes look at hdx-evals, the open-source framework we built to benchmark the ClickStack MCP server against a raw SQL baseline using deterministic synthetic incidents, sandboxed Claude agents, and blind LLM grading — and why the MCP scored hi

item.title: RAG debugging guide: fast ways to reduce retrieval errors | Redis Blog
item.description: Your RAG-backed support assistant just told a customer the refund window is 30 days. It's 14. The retrieval logs look clean: chunks came back, latency was normal, nothing errored. That's what makes RA
item.content:encoded: Your RAG-backed support assistant just told a customer the refund window is 30 days. It's 14. The retrieval logs look clean: chunks came back, latency was normal, nothing errored. That's what makes RAG failures slippery. The pipeline still returns an ...
item.author: Jeff Mills

_custom.originalTitle: RAG debugging guide: fast ways to reduce retrieval errors

item.title: Migrate Datadog telemetry with the OpenTelemetry Collector | ClickHouse Blog
item.description: Recent improvements to the OpenTelemetry Collector's Datadog receiver let teams reroute telemetry from their existing Datadog agents and SDKs to ClickStack. or any OTel destination making migrations a
item.content:encoded: Recent improvements to the OpenTelemetry Collector's Datadog receiver let teams reroute telemetry from their existing Datadog agents and SDKs to ClickStack. or any OTel destination making migrations and evaluations simpler than ever.

_custom.originalTitle: Migrate Datadog telemetry with the OpenTelemetry Collector

item.title: PostgresBench: Measuring the impact of High Availability on Managed Postgres performance | ClickHouse Blog
item.description: PostgresBench now includes HA benchmarks, comparing the performance of managed Postgres services under production-style HA configurations. This post explains the architectures behind each service and
item.content:encoded: PostgresBench now includes HA benchmarks, comparing the performance of managed Postgres services under production-style HA configurations. This post explains the architectures behind each service and analyzes the trade-offs between availability guarantees

_custom.originalTitle: PostgresBench: Measuring the impact of High Availability on Managed Postgres performance

item.title: ClickHouse announced as Principal Partner and Front of Shirt Sponsor of Fulham Football Club | ClickHouse Blog
item.description: Fulham Football Club is proud to announce ClickHouse, a global technology company specialising in real-time analytics, data infrastructure and AI applications, as its newest Principal Partner and Fron
item.content:encoded: Fulham Football Club is proud to announce ClickHouse, a global technology company specialising in real-time analytics, data infrastructure and AI applications, as its newest Principal Partner and Front of Shirt Sponsor of the Men’s First Team.

_custom.originalTitle: ClickHouse announced as Principal Partner and Front of Shirt Sponsor of Fulham Football Club

item.title: Query Profiling: See Where a Slow Query Spends Its Time | Weaviate Blog
item.description: When a Weaviate query is slow, the first question is where the time went. Query profiling returns a per-stage, per-shard timing breakdown, making query performance issues visible.
item.content:encoded: When a Weaviate query is slow, the first question is where the time went. Query profiling returns a per-stage, per-shard timing breakdown, making query performance issues visible.

_custom.originalTitle: Query Profiling: See Where a Slow Query Spends Its Time

item.title: Cache consistency: strategies to keep data fresh | Redis Blog
item.description: A cache that has drifted from your database will happily serve wrong prices, expired permissions, or phantom inventory, and it won't feel a shred of guilt about it. Cache consistency is the discipline
item.content:encoded: A cache that has drifted from your database will happily serve wrong prices, expired permissions, or phantom inventory, and it won't feel a shred of guilt about it. Cache consistency is the discipline behind keeping that drift small, so cached values ...
item.author: Jeff Mills

_custom.originalTitle: Cache consistency: strategies to keep data fresh

item.title: client/v3.0.0-beta | Release notes from milvus
item.description: client/v3.0.0-betaThis is the first beta release of the Milvus Go SDK v3, targeting Milvus 3.0. It is compared against client/v2.6.5.This is a beta release. APIs and protocol details may change before
item.content:encoded: client/v3.0.0-betaThis is the first beta release of the Milvus Go SDK v3, targeting Milvus 3.0. It is compared against client/v2.6.5.This is a beta release. APIs and protocol details may change before v3.0.0 GA.HighlightsMigrates the Go module to github.com/milvus-io/milvus/client/v3.Decouples the standalone client from Milvus server-side pkg modules, substantially reducing server-only transitive dependencies.Adds collection snapshot management and asynchronous restore workflows.Adds read-only e

enclosure.alt:
client/v3.0.0-beta This is the first beta release of the Milvus Go SDK v3, targeting Milvus 3.0. It is compared against client/v2.6.5.
This is a beta release. APIs and protocol details may change ...

_custom.originalTitle: client/v3.0.0-beta
